rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VEOLSIE YOUNG HOPKINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents First American Title Insurance Co., First American Title Co., B.K. Powell And N.Powell'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgement On The Pleadings On Petitioner;S Fourth And Fifth Causes Of Action.
rulings.Rulings: Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings filed by Respondents First American and the Powells on Petitioner Young's Fourth and Fifth Causes of Action in the First Amended Verified Petition. tentative ruling: The court finds that Petitioner had proper notice for the Motion for Summary Adjudication under CCP 437c. In light of the court's tentative ruling on the Motion for Summary Adjudication and in light of footnote number 1 in Respondent's Memorandum of Points and Authorities in support of Judgment on the Pleadings, the Judgment on the Pleadings motion is moot, and therefore shall not be considered.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VEOLSIE YOUNG HOPKINS
rulings.Rulings: 1st motion Motion for summary adjudication of issues filed by Respondents First American Title Insurance Company, First American Title (First American), Brian K. Powell and Nicole Powell (Powells) as to the First Amended Verified Petition filed by William J. Young on 12/7/09. issue No. 1 on the 4th cause of action for financial elder abuse against the Powells and First American on the grounds that the undisputed facts demonstrate respondents did not engage in any elder abuse. tentative ruling as to the Powells: hearing required for proof of the Powells' bankruptcy stay. tentative ruling as to First American: grant. No triable issue of material fact as to financial elder abuse. There is no evidence that First American took, secreted, appropriated, or retained real or personal property of Hopkins, an elder adult, to a wrongful use or with an intent to defraud, or both, or assisted in doing any of those acts. issue No. 2 on the 5th cause of action for Breach of Title Insurance Policy and Breach of Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing against First American on the grounds that the undisputed facts show petitioner is not a party to and/or an intended third party beneficiary of any contract with respondents. tentative ruling: Grant. Petitioner is not a party to the title insurance policy between First American and the Powells or a third party beneficiary of said title insurance policy. See - Kenny v Safeco Title Ins. Co. 113 Cal. App. 3d 557 (1980). issue No. 3 on the 2nd cause of action to Quiet Title (adverse possession) against the Powells tentative ruling: hearing required for proof of the Powells' bankruptcy stay. Evidentiary objections: Petitioner William J. Young's sole objection to evidence proffered by First American - overrule All other evidentiary objections raised by Petitioner Young in his opposing separate statement are overruled because they are not in compliance with CRC 3.1354(b). rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing And Petition By Respondents Wachovia Mortgage Ana Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. For Award Of Attorneys; Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities.
